answer:
Purpose
One of the most effective ways to troubleshoot Power PDF issues is by collecting logs for our support team to review.
Before beginning, ensure that the File Name extension option is selected under the View option in Folder Options in Windows Explorer.
Open Folder
à
View
à
Check mark view File name extensions
Objective:
When Power PDF logging is needed, the log files for both x32 and x64 bit processing should be enabled
Step 1
Browse to the default directory where Power PDF software is installed on your system.
Typically, the file location would be:
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025
Step 2
2.1 If you see a file by name recdiag_log.ini with size 1 KB you may rename the file to recdiag_log_old.ini
2.2 Search and select RECDIAG_LOG.IN_ of size 13 KB at location C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025
then rename the file to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini'.
Note
– In some cases, renaming this file will require admin rights, but this depends on the customers environment and user permissions
32-Bit log steps
To also enable x86-bit logging mode, continue with the steps below and first b
rowse to the directory where Power PDF is installed on your system.
Typically, the file location would be C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025\x86
Step 3
If you see a file by name recdiag_log.ini with a size of 1 KB you may rename the file to recdiag_log_old.ini
Step 4
Search and select RECDIAG_LOG.IN_ of size 13 KB at location
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025\x86
then rename the file to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini'.
Step 5
5.1 Once the 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file is in place in both locations, create a folder named 'RECDIAG' in the C:\TEMP folder for the logs to be written out to
5.2 If this folder is not manually created before testing, the logs will not be created (C:\temp\recdiag).
5.3 Power PDF should start writing logs to C:\temp\recdiag as soon as the software is launched.
Step 6 -
Reproduce the issue and note the document name, date and time of the issue.
Collect the logs from C:\temp\recdiag after reproducing the issue successfully.
Share the logs with our support team.
Step 7
When testing is complete, be sure to rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' back to 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' so Power PDF does not continue logging,

Question / Problem:
How to reopen a closed Support Case?
Answer / Solution:
Support Cases are closed sometimes automatically by the Support Portal Software. This happens if, after a certain period, no feedback is received from our customer. Closed Cases can be reopened 14 days after that they have been closed. If the Case is older then 14 days, you will have to open a new Case. You can refer to the old Case by the old Case Number.
To reopen a closed Case, you need to login to your Kofax Support Account and click on Cases and then on My Cases.
Here choose "My Closed Cases" from the menu on the left hand side.
Search for the closed Case in question and click on the Case Number. The closed Case will be shown. Click on "Re-open closed Case" to reopen the closed case.

answer:
Necessary requirements for remote sessions involving access to end customers' server where Tungsten Automation software is installed. These requirements ensure smooth communication, effective troubleshooting, and compliance with contractual obligations.
1. Access Requirements:
Remote Access
: Partners/customers should provide reasonable remote access to the servers where Tungsten Automation software is installed.
Level of Control
: While full control may not always be granted due to security concerns, partners/customers should allow for a level of access that enables effective troubleshooting and support.
Options for Access
: This may include direct access to the servers, utilizing a jump box or sub-system designated for support purposes, or consideration of providing a jump box by the customer for remote access.
2. Knowledge of Software:
All Parties
: The designated contact person should possess sufficient knowledge of Tungsten Automation software to administer it and fulfill support obligations.
Partner Support:
The designated case contact is required to be properly trained/certified in Tungsten Automation software to administer and fulfill support obligations, including but not limited to, driving remote sessions.
Direct Support:
The designated case contact should possess sufficient knowledge in Tungsten Automation software to administer and fulfill support obligations, including but not limited to, driving remote sessions.
3. Minimum Requirements for Remote Sessions:
Communication Channels
: Ensure clear communication channels (e.g., chat, voice call) are established prior to the remote session.
Screen Sharing
: Partners/customers should be able to share their screen to facilitate visual inspection and troubleshooting.
Permission Levels
: Define the permission levels required for different types of remote access (e.g., view-only access, control access).
Preferred Tools
: The preferred tools for remote sessions include:
TeamViewer
Microsoft Teams with Remote Access control
Windows Quick Assist
WebEx
4. Disclaimers:
Requirements
: If the above requirements are not met, the company may be relieved from its obligations until all requirements are met if deemed critical.
Discussion and Resolution
: During discussions regarding access limitations, partners/customers should maintain a level of control while accommodating the company's requests to the extent possible.
This KB serves as a reference guide for all parties involved in remote sessions, emphasizing the importance of collaboration, communication, and adherence to contractual obligations.

and

To resolve this issue, follow one of the two solutions below:
Solution 1: Apply Registry Fix Manually
Back Up the Registry
:
Open the Windows Registry Editor by pressing
Win + R
, typing
regedit
, and pressing Enter.
Before making any changes, back up the registry:
Go to
File > Export
.
Save the backup in a secure location.
2. Create the Missing Registry Keys
:
Open a text editor (e.g., Notepad) and paste the following content:
Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\PFU\ScanSnap Extension\Tungsten Power PDF Advanced]
@="C:\\Program Files\\Tungsten\\Power PDF 2025\\ScanSnapAdapterU.exe"
"Adapter"="C:\\Program Files\\Tungsten\\Power PDF 2025\\bin\\PowerPDF.exe"
"Config"="C:\\ProgramData\\Kofax\\PDF\\V1\\ScanSnap.ini"
"Path"="C:\\Program Files\\Tungsten\\Power PDF 2025"
3. Save and Import Registry File
:
Save the file as
ScanSnapFix.reg
.
Double-click the saved
.reg
file to import it into the Windows Registry.
Confirm any prompts to apply the changes.
4. Restart the Computer
:
Restart the computer to ensure the registry changes take effect.
5. Test the Scanner
:
Open Power PDF 2025.3 and verify that the Fujitsu ScanSnap scanners are now visible and functional.
Solution 2: Update Tungsten Power PDF to Fix Compatibility
Tungsten Automation released
Power PDF Business 2025.3 Update 2
, which addresses the issue where ScanSnap iX500 scanners were not detected.
Download the Update
:
Visit the official Tungsten Power PDF release notes:
Power PDF 2025.3.2 Update
.
Install the Update
:
Follow the instructions provided in the update documentation to install the patch.
Test the Scanner
:
Verify that the Fujitsu ScanSnap scanners are now working with the updated version of Power PDF.

answer:
When logging is needed for Power PDF 2025.3, log files for both 64 and 32 bit processing should be collected.
64-Bit:
To enable 64 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
then rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the installation folder which is typically "C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025".
In some cases, renaming this file will require admin rights, but this depends on the customers environment and user permissions
32-Bit:
To enable 32 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the x64 folder within the installation folder which is typically " C:\Program Files\Tungsten\Power PDF 2025\x86".
Once the 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file is in place in both locations, create a folder named 'RECDIAG' in the C:\TEMP folder for the logs to be written.
If this folder is not manually created before testing, the logs will not be created (C:\temp\recdiag).
Power PDF should start writing logs to C:\temp\recdiag as soon as the software is launched.
When testing is complete, be sure to rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' back to 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' so Power PDF does not continue logging, and send those to the support team

ISSUE:
How to show the Microsoft Purview Information Protection label notification bar - by default not shown after fixpack 5.1 fixpack 10 or later updates are applied - and this cannot be controlled within the network customization tool kit.
Where is this stored?
SOLUTION:
Microsoft Purview Information Protection is a feature that is enabled in the base install and can be leveraged.
You can check your Security tab to see if it is there (see below) and when configured, we only prompt the label and user for authentication beyond fixpack 10 updates or later is configured and deployed for Power PDF 5.1 *and when they open an encypted document.
A PDF itself saved with only a sensitivty label will not prompt or show a label.
There is a registry key, which we expect it to be set this way [out of the box; hiding the label banner]
[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Kofax\PDF\Kofax Power PDF\Preference]
"ShowNotificaton"=dword:00000000 [Disabled]
Enabled would should look like this; and this key can be pushed out to impact the checkbox in the below screenshot:
"ShowNotificaton"=dword:00000001 [Enabled]
This has been also proposed to be added to the network customization tool kit but it currently has to be set via the current user registry since the new features were introduced in late 2025 with the power PDF 5.1 and 2025.3 releases [for business] offering improved Microsoft Purview document encryption abilities and features for the product.
After reviewing this and planning the deployment; please open a support ticket should further questions need to be reviewed on Microsoft Purview document encryption workflows.

Create Two Required Models in Microsoft Foundry:
Chat Deployment Model:

gpt-4o

text-embedding-ada-002

Test the Integration:
Attempt to deploy the Azure AI agent again. The errors should be resolved if the models are correctly configured and located within the same resource.

Starting with the FixPack 8 of Power PDF v5.1, the release notes in ReadMe-TungstenPowerPDFBusiness-5.1.1.8.htm, under the section "Special Instructions," provide guidance on the solution for the problem.
Understanding Digital Signature Expiration and Trusted Publishers in Microsoft Office Add-ins
When dealing with digital signatures in the context of Microsoft Office add-ins, it is important to understand how expiration periods work. The expiration date of a signature refers to the window in which the signature can be used to sign files. However, this expiration does not mean that the signed add-in is no longer valid after that date.
Time Stamps and Long-Term Validity:
In practice, time stamps are often used alongside digital signatures to ensure that the signature remains verifiable even after the expiration of the signing certificate. A time stamp records the exact moment the signing occurred, ensuring the file or add-in can still be validated long after the signing certificate has expired.
Context in Microsoft Office Add-ins:
When the
Require Application Add-ins to be signed by Trusted Publishers
setting is enabled, Office checks that the add-in’s digital signature matches a trusted certificate, to ensure that only verified, secure code is executed.
Adding Certificates to Trusted Publishers:
To ensure that Microsoft Word recognizes your add-ins as safe, the signing certificate should be added to the list of trusted publishers. Even if the certificate has expired, Word will still trust the signed add-in as long as the certificate was valid at the time of signing.
When the Office feature for trusted publishers is enabled, it is important to install the certificate included with each new fix pack into the trusted publishers repository.
Follow the steps below to manually add the certificate:
Double-click the
.cer
file that comes with the fix pack.
Click
Install Certificate
.
Choose
Current User
or
Local Machine
depending on your preference, then click
Next
.
Select
Place all certificates in the following store
, then click
Browse
and select
"Trusted Publishers"
, then click
OK
.
Click
Next
, then
Finish
to confirm and complete the certificate import.

Understand that flattening in Power PDF only removes stamps from comments but does not make them non-editable objects.
To create a truly non-editable, image-only version of the document, print the document to the Power PDF printer. This process rasterizes the content, preventing further editing.

answer:
Our default design when power PDF is installed to have the destination set to "query the filename" on its drop down, which means the user is asked to point to save a PDF document manually after sending a print job with -save as popping up.
This can be customized on one machine, and then the settings are stored in .ini files that are normally found in this location: "%APPDATA%\Kofax\PDF\PDF Driver\ENU\Destination.ini"
The File looks like this
[Destination]
NamingMode=0
PDFName=
FileExistRule=0
bMailDelivery=0
MailRecipient=
MergeRule=00
MergeOrder=0
MergeRepeat=0
** NamingMode=
X
this is where the default PDF destination is defined
0 - Query the filename
1 - Specify filename below
2 - Save in named folder; prompt filename
3 - Save in named folder; auto-name folder
4 - Mail result only, do not save the file
5 - Save to DMS
6 - Send to PaperPort
7 - Send to PowerPDF
FileExistRule=X - this is where the file exists rules are set
0 - Show Warning Dialog
1 - Replace existing file
2 - Add trailing number to filename
3 - Insert at Start of existing file
4 - Append to existing file
5 - Overlay with existing file
PDF Name or Folder is stored in PDFName="value"
This file can be captured and pushed out to new user profiles as part of a deployment or login script to pre-populate the destination tab of the settings.

question: Known CVE's in Power PDF

answer:
Uninstall Power PDF from the machine, and redeploy with adjusted syntax to add the activation type required and the installation will complete without this issuel
MSIEXEC.EXE /i "Kofax Power PDF Advanced.msi" ACTMODE="SN" ISX_SERIALNUM="AV09Z-xxx-xxxx-xxxx-xx" /QN
Level of Complexity
High
question: Power PDF v5 30 days grace period appears when performing an command line silent install using the legacy serial number

answer:
Fixpack 5.1.1.8
936690
: When archiving an email to PDF using Power PDF, some text was missing, and some was inverted (file-specific).
Note: there is a difference converting .MSG and .EML files outside of Outlook. This is caused by the way Microsoft parses the file. Question:
How many days does it take for an activated power PDF for business license to be released back into the pool; if the current machine stops launching the software within the environment communicating with an server installation of the KLS on Windows server
Solution:
If Power PDF is installed and contacts the license server (happens on launch) at least once, it activates and then will show in the license server usage reports. Later If used frequently during the next weeks it will renew the session with the license server via every 15 days.
If used only the one time, after the 15 day period has passed it won’t renew the session if never launched on the same machine. Then after another 15 days passes which will be 30 days, on the server side it will automatically be released from license server and the seat will be made available for consumption by another user.
Troubleshooting Reference point:
The license manager C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 51\PPDFLM.exe is the responsible code module to communicate and ensure this handshake happens after the period; however If there is security software or firewall blocking communication between the server PC and the client with this module then the 'grace' period may appear to the user in power PDF which will countdown to when the software will become unlicensed in trial mode.
There is an option in the design to set an license allow List which is saved as a plain-text file (NamedUsersList.txt) placed in the common application data folder. By default, the full path is: C:\ProgramData\Kofax\Licensing\NamedUsersList.txt
By default, if no Allow List file is set so the KLS License Server provides licenses for Power PDF upon request from any user
For further information on this specific configuration; please review page 80 of the network installation guide linked below which applies to version 5.1 and 6.6.1 of the license server as of writing this article:
https://docshield.tungstenautomation.com/PowerPDF/en_US/5.1.1-x2ki7a3ycc/print/TungstenPowerPDFNetworkInstallationGuide_EN.pdf

Kofax Power PDF License Types – Enterprise Overview
1. Legacy License
- Type: Traditional perpetual or volume license key.
- Deployment: Installed and activated locally on each machine using a license key.
- Management: Minimal centralized control – each installation is managed individually.
- Activation: Manual or Automation over the internet (offline activation possible).
2. Kofax License Server (KLS)
- Type: On-premises license management.
- Deployment: Requires installation of the Kofax License Server within the organization’s network. Can deploy a primary and secondary license server for redundancy
- Management: Centralized – distributes licenses to clients in the network.
- Activation: Licenses are checked out/in dynamically from the internal server.
-Server activation: Manual or Automation over the internet (offline activation possible).
3. Cloud License Server (CLS)
- Type: Cloud-based licensing model.
- Deployment: Managed through Kofax's cloud infrastructure – no on-premises license server needed.
- Management: Centralized via the cloud – admins can manage user access and license distribution remotely.
- Activation: Requires internet access – clients communicate with the cloud to obtain licenses.

Issue:
Unable to scan from Power PDF on Windows Build 24H2.
Cause:
Windows Build 24H2 was released on October 1, 2024. This update overrides existing scanner drivers and applies the WSD (Web Services for Devices) driver, which is Microsoft's proprietary driver.
Power PDF supports universal scanning interfaces such as TWAIN, WIA, WIA2, and ISI. However, WSD is not one of the supported interfaces.
Solution:
This is not an issue with Power PDF. Windows Build 24H2 prevents users from utilizing supported scanner drivers such as TWAIN, WIA, and WIA2.
Users are advised to contact their scanner manufacturers and Microsoft for further assistance.

, and go to the

answer:
1: Remove drive letter with Command Prompt
The command you’ll perform will allow you to connect and disconnect drive letter to a virtual location.
Disconnecting a letter should allow your program to install without generating an error message. So, here’s what you need to do:
Go to Search, type cmd and open Command Prompt
In the Command Prompt, type the following line and press Enter:
subst H: C:\ then press enter. (replace H with actual Drive Letter)
Close Command Prompt and try to install Power PDF.
Solution 2: Disconnect network drive
This solution will work only if a network drive appears in This PC, after you’ve seen the error otherwise stick with the previous solution.
Follow these steps to disconnect the network drive in order to eliminate error 1327:
Go to Search, type file explorer and open File Explorer
Go to This PC
Press ctrl + F1 in order to open ribbon with tools
Under Map networking drive go to Disconnect network drive
Try to install Power PDF again
Solution 3: Reassociate the drive letter with a temporary location
This method allows you to link a drive letter to a virtual drive location. In this manner, the installer will locate the respective temporary path and use it to install the problematic software.
Note
: If the temporary path doesn’t exist, you need to create it.
Launch Command Prompt as an administrator
Enter subst [Driver Letter]: C:temp > hit Enter
Close Command Prompt > reinstall the software.
Launch Command Prompt again and enter the command: subst /D [Driver Letter]: to remove the software’s association with the temporary path > hit Enter.
Level of Complexity
Moderate

Advisory: Users of Power PDF imanage connector connecting with iManage work 10 cloud will now be unable to open a PDF to check out inside of powerPDF or save new documents back to the document management system if these older versions are in use.
ISSUE Scope:
affects all hotfixes and fixpacks of PowerPDF v3.1 and v4.0 with iManage work 10 cloud
CAUSE
On or before August 21st 2023, iManage completed a API change to V2 universal API at the platform they host for customers of iManage cloud. The root change was that they have fully deprecated browser controls using Chromium and legacy internet explorer 11 browsers and this means older integrations now within the open and save screen can not load correctly.
In short this means the only platform that is supported requires MSedge Webview 2 to work further.
SOLUTION:
Install power PDF 4.1 - Fixpack 2 or higher OR Power PDF 5.0 which natively contains the webview2 technologies they require for the integration to function
Also suggested; to update all the native iManage client software to the most recent version past 10.6 when testing this
.
Please refer to the below network information guides clickable at the below link which contain imanage V2 server authentication requirements for configuration to be completed.
a) 5.0
https://docshield.kofax.com/Portal/Products/en_US/PowerPDF/5.0.0-3uoz7ssq2b/PowerPDF.htm
b) 4.1 -
https://docshield.kofax.com/Portal/Products/en_US/PowerPDF/4.1.0-7qjur27uhf/PowerPDF.htm
Once you have these higher kofax versions installed at the client side the issue will be resolved.
Background information:
iManage support has warned about this 2 years ago in the following URL advisory article they are planning platform changes in advance.
URL:
https://help.imanage.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260806477829-ADVISORY-Work-Desktop-for-Windows-will-no-longer-support-the-use-of-the-Microsoft-Internet-Explorer-web-
With the iManage Work Desktop 10.5 for Windows June 2021 release, the web browser control used for authentication and user interface display in Work Desktop for Windows will change to the Microsoft Edge WebView2 control. The user interface display includes the following:
File picker (Open, Save, and Select) dialog boxes
Task panes (Timeline, Properties, Versions, and Filing Details)
Co-authoring dialog boxes
In early September 2021 was when Kofax PowerPDF v4.1 as well moved towards this new universal API to prevent issues going without webview2 in place. This was implemented in Fix Pack 2 (KofaxPowerPDFAdvanced-4.1.0.2) and higher, installation of the Microsoft Edge WebView2 control is required by the connector to connect going forward so we were proactive on this integration.
Please get in touch with
Tungsten Automation Technical Support
should assistance be required on configuration

answer:
1-PPDFLM.exe
Purpose:
This executable is the License Manager for Power PDF.
Usage:
It manages and validates the licensing information for the application, ensuring compliance with licensing terms.
When Power PDF is launched, this process may communicate with a license server or access locally stored license files to verify the user's entitlement.
Why it might trigger ASR detections:
License management tools often interact with system files, registry entries, and external servers to perform validation tasks.
These actions, while legitimate, can resemble suspicious behavior to ASR mechanisms, potentially leading to false positives.
2-PowerPDF.exe
Purpose:
This is the main executable for Power PDF.
Usage:
It serves as the primary application for opening, editing, creating, and managing PDF files.
The process is also responsible for integrating with Microsoft Office products, such as converting Word documents to PDFs or annotating PDFs embedded within Office files.
Why it might trigger ASR detections:
The interaction with Office files, particularly during conversions or integrations, can be flagged by ASR.
Processes involving macros, scripts, or unusual access patterns may appear suspicious, even if they are part of legitimate workflows.
3-SendMail.exe
Purpose:
This executable handles email-related functionalities within Power PDF.
Usage:
It enables users to send PDF files directly via email from within the application, leveraging their configured email client.
This feature is often used in workflows where a user edits a PDF and shares it immediately with colleagues or clients.
Why it might trigger ASR detections:
Executables that interact with email clients, access local or network files, or utilize network resources can resemble potentially malicious activity.
Features involving scripting or automation, even when used legitimately, might be flagged by ASR as suspicious.

answer:
Issue:
Can external participants outside the organization be invited to use the Online Collaboration method in Power PDF?
Solution:
No, the Online Collaboration method in Power PDF does not support inviting external participants outside the organization unless specific conditions are met. Here is a detailed explanation of the collaboration methods available in Power PDF:
The collaboration feature in Power PDF supports two methods:
1-Local Network Collaboration:
Enables real-time PDF collaboration over a secure local network without requiring internet access, access on the same subnet
2-Online Collaboration Using an On-Premise Server:
Facilitates remote PDF collaboration via a centrally managed, secure on-premise server which can cover any subnet location on the same network
External participants cannot be invited unless they are part of the same domain and can access the collaborative server hosted on-premise.
Access for external participants requires a VPN connection or other supported methods provided by the operating system to securely connect to the server.
Reference:
For detailed instructions and technical guidance, refer to the following resources:
Network Installer Guide:
See page 71 for information on configuring and using the collaboration feature.
Network Installation Guide
Collaboration Meeting Documentation:
Access detailed help on collaboration features and functionality.
Collaboration Feature Documentation

Below is information I got for a client that was interested in PowerPDF Mobile with OneDrive (Not Supported, but there is a work around below).
Unfortunately, the Power PDF mobile app for iOS does not natively support OneDrive, meaning it cannot directly access files stored in the OneDrive cloud.
To access OneDrive files, Power PDF relies on the OneDrive integration within the iOS file picker:
However, if a user tries to open a file from OneDrive using the iOS file picker, and the file is not yet downloaded to the mobile device memory, the OneDrive client should first download it before allowing the application to access the downloaded version. But unfortunately, we have also noticed that sometimes OneDrive integration fails to load the files on-the-fly, and the user is getting the following error ("Cannot open this document"):
This issue similarly affects other applications as well:
Some showing a similar error message:
Some are just hanging forever on the Opening... :
Even within the native iOS file picker app, "Files" (it unable to load and render the PDF, only a short file summary is available in such a case):
As a workaround, we suggest making those files or folders available offline in the OneDrive application first:
Please Note, Power PDF mobile app for iOS can natively support Dropbox or cloud storages that support the WebDAV protocol:
Below is some information for Android:
We do support Android and that it can be deployed via Intune like IOS can; it is just not documented in the network install guide for Intune because we do not have customized settings like iOS today to 'prepopulate' the options, but the process is possible like any other android application.

Details about Sendmail.exe in PowerPDF
iManage system leverages the rest API v1 in 2019-2021 (legacy integrations) and the v2 universal Rest API in the recent releases after 2021, until today (Nov 8, 2024).
Power PDF 3.1 was released in April 2019; so it lacks support for the v2 APIs mentioned.
Post man is a utility specifically for the restAPI > think of it as the SQL management studio which aids you to write queries for the restAPI
All power PDF releases include a executable of code called 'sendmail.exe' located in the bin folder, depending on which version.
C:\Program Files
(x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 51\bin\SendMail.exe
This 'sendmail.exe' is called only when using PDF create > in word = clicking on 'create pdf and email' / in excel / in power Point /
or printing to the power PDF driver with the 'Destination set as 'Mail the result only'
The sendmail.exe should only be responsible for receiving a converted document from our API's and do a MAPI call or modal/ to call the default email application on the system and create and attach the PDF document converted.
An issue with excessive API calls is generated based on user traffic and if 3.1 is involved; it is caused by, which only supports the V1 restAPI + requires a customized imWorkOptions.xml configuration different that 4.x and 5.x power PDF users and these need to be uncommented in the XML file
The copy which gets downloaded from the server will be located in the profile %APPDATA%\Roaming\iManage\Work\Configs.
To customize it locally, you can edit the file and copy to %LOCALAPPDATA%\iManage\Work\Configs.
4.1, 5.0, 5.1:
<ws_option name="integration">
<key name="IntegratedProcesses" value="PowerPDF"/>
</ws_option>
3.1
<ws_option name="integration">
<key name="IntegratedProcesses" value="NuancePDF"/>
</ws_option>

answer:
Knowledge Article:
Adding Page Numbers to a Document Created by Combining Multiple Smaller Documents with Pre-Existing Page Numbers
Issue:
Incorrect page numbers are added when rearranging a document created by combining multiple smaller documents that already have page numbers. For example, the first page may be numbered as 39 or another incorrect number.
Solution:
To resolve this issue, you must flatten all smaller documents before combining them into a single large document. Flattening will remove any existing dynamic elements like footers or page numbers, allowing you to add new, consistent page numbers throughout the combined document.
Step-by-Step Guide:
How to Flatten a Document:
Open the document in Power PDF.
Navigate to the Advanced Processing tab.
Select Flatten File.
In the Flatten popup, check the Select All option.
Click OK to proceed.
Save the flattened document in your preferred location.
How to Combine Files After Flattening:
Open Power PDF and go to the Home tab.
Select Combine Files.
In the Create PDF prompt, click +Add.
Browse to the location where the flattened files are saved, and select all the flattened documents.
Click the Play button to combine the files.
Save the combined file in your preferred location.
Reference:
This knowledge article is based on common troubleshooting steps for addressing page number issues in combined PDF files. These best practices are recommended by Tungsten Automation support specialists.

answer:
When logging is needed for Power PDF 5.0, log files for both x32 and x64 bit processing should be collected.
32-Bit:
To enable x32 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
then rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the installation folder which is typically "C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 51".
In some cases, renaming this file will require admin rights, but this depends on the customers environment and user permissions
64-Bit:
To enable x64 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the x64 folder within the installation folder which is typically " C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 51\x64".
Once the 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file is in place in both locations, create a folder named 'RECDIAG' in the C:\TEMP folder for the logs to be written.
If this folder is not manually created before testing, the logs will not be created (C:\temp\recdiag).
Power PDF should start writing logs to C:\temp\recdiag as soon as the software is launched.
When testing is complete, be sure to rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' back to 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' so Power PDF does not continue logging, and send those to the support team

answer:
ISSUE:
Users encounter difficulty changing the default PDF reader from Microsoft Edge to Kofax in Windows 11, resulting in the need to manually select Kofax each time.
CAUSE:
The default app settings in Windows 11 may not update properly, leading to Microsoft Edge retaining its status as the default PDF reader despite user preferences.
SOLUTION:
Check Default App Settings:
Open Settings using Windows key + I.
Navigate to "Apps" > "Default apps" > "Choose default apps by file type."
Select ".pdf" and choose Kofax as the preferred PDF reader.
Proceed to "Set defaults by application" and ensure Kofax is set as the default for .pdf files.
In "Recommended browser settings," uncheck both options to prevent Edge from reclaiming default status.
Ensure Third-Party Applications are Updated:
Ensure that Kofax and other third-party applications are updated to their latest versions.
Repair Microsoft Edge:
Open the Run dialog with Windows key + R, type "appwiz.cpl," and press Enter.
Right-click on Edge > "Change" > "Repair."
Confirm that Kofax remains the default PDF reader after repair.
Troubleshoot Edge:
In Edge, click on the three dots > "Settings" > "Reset Settings."
Check for updates by clicking "About Microsoft Edge."
Consider removing extensions and clearing the cache to resolve potential conflicts.
Reinstall Microsoft Edge:
If necessary, reinstall Edge by visiting the official Microsoft website and downloading the latest version.

to fix this issue uninstall powerPDF v5, and if fix packs has been applied, run the remover tool next and reboot the machine; only after rebooting then reinstall the software which can be the base power PDFv5 and the fixpack in one go.
Set up exclusions for our integration windows explorer contextual right click menu components required to be allowed to run
1) C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 50\Bin\SDirectShellExt.dll
2) C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 50\ShellExt.dll
3) C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 50\cnvsts_x64.dll
^ Also Verify that the above files are present in the power PDF folder, and if so; they can be registered in windows or command line as a workaround.
Command: Regsvr32 [/u] [/n] [/i[:cmdline]] <dllname>
/u - Unregister server (use for unregistering after installations)
/i - Call DllInstall passing it an optional [cmdline];
/s – Silent; display no message boxes (to allow registering silently)

Finally if the issue is impacting end users, it can be avoided entirely also by disabling windows explorer integration with powerPDF at deployment time so for all users pushed out by command line or transforms MST file, SCCM or the wizard <custom> setup option deployment method.
If this is the trigger identified and no actions attempted will resolve the issue and stop it from reoccurring please contact Tungsten Automation technical support; so that our team can provide direction and support reviewing the installation deployment method, scripting and MSI logs from an impacted workstation.

and create a new DWORD entry with the name
EnableLinkedConnections
and value
1

All users must have access to this location and stamps to do this configuration, so If the software is already installed, you can set this
centralized location feature by editing the following file: "C:\ProgramData\Kofax\PDF\PowerPDF\Preferences\ProductConfig.ini".
All you need to do is open this file in notepad and at the end you should see the STAMP section; here add the UNC path to the stamps folder on your network folder; and have them made available for a wider audience of users.
EXAMPLE:
[Stamp]
UseCommonStamp=1
CENTRALIZED_LOCATION=\\RO-MDYER-LT\temp
Stamps should be stored in this location in a folder named "\ENU"
so in this configuration the stamps would be moved here: \\RO-MDYER-LT\temp\ENU\

ISSUE:
User testing with Power PDF to open an Azure Information Protection (AIP) encrypted documents. AIP files are getting an "empty authorization token" after three attempts pop up in Power PDF. This is due to TLS 1.2 being disabled.
SOLUTION:
This specific PC was affected due to the a change in security settings for TLS;
In case you experience issues with authorization to AIP from Power PDF, please configure the .NET Framework for strong cryptography (to support TLS 1.2).
Please note, this a per machine setting and may have effect on other software installed to this machine that uses .NET Framework.
Apply the following reg keys:
For 32-bit applications that are running on 32-bit OSs and for 64-bit applications that are running on 64-bit OSs, update the following subkey values:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727]
"SystemDefaultTlsVersions" = dword:00000001
"SchUseStrongCrypto" = dword:00000001
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319]
"SystemDefaultTlsVersions" = dword:00000001
"SchUseStrongCrypto" = dword:00000001
For 32-bit applications that are running on 64-bit OSs, update the following subkey values:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727]
"SystemDefaultTlsVersions" = dword:00000001
"SchUseStrongCrypto" = dword:00000001
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319]
"SystemDefaultTlsVersions" = dword:00000001
"SchUseStrongCrypto" = dword:00000001

question: Unable to preview PDF files in Windows 10 Explorer or inside MS Outlook with Power PDF 4.1

answer:
First, check in word or excel or outlook to see if the Addon itself is 'unloaded' or inactive add-ins or enable the disabled add-ins .
depending on the state of the issue the follow should be checked.
Check the Load Behavior Key - 2 means always load the addon at start up
Open Regedit and go to: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\16\Word\Addins\ < select the affected addon> and check the value - if LoadBehavior was set to "0" - Change this to "3" which is load at startup and should work correctly next time.
CAUTION:
Using Regedit is not recommended for inexperienced computer users.
EVEN EXPERIENCED USERS SHOULD CREATE A NEW SYSTEM RESTORE POINT BEFORE MAKING ANY CHANGES IN THE REGISTRY.
IF YOU FIND YOU'VE MADE AN ERROR, YOU CAN USE THAT RESTORE POINT TO RETURN THE REGISTRY TO THE WAY IT WAS PRIOR TO MAKING THAT CHANGE.
Solution:
in word - click on On the File tab, option > on the left side choose Add-ins and then click on Manage: Com Add-ins
click Slow and Disabled COM Add-ins.
You'll see a list of add-ins. Click
Always enable this add-in for the following
NPDFCOfficeAddin.NPDFOffice.1
NPDFOfficeAddin.OfficeAddin
NExportPDF.ExportAddin
If the LoadBehavior is anything other than "3" then follow these steps:
1) Close Word (or Excel or Outlook). Make sure the "winword.exe" process is not running in the Windows Task Manager.
2) Open the registry editor again. Double-click the registry key to edit it.
3) Change the Load Behavior to "3." Close the registry editor.
4) Re-open the registry editor to ensure "3" is still there.
5) Open Word. The add-in should now load automatically at startup and the user will see the Kofax tab.
Add-in Resiliency
If changing the LoadBehavior key still does not resolve it,
there is another registry key you can adjust:
Microsoft keeps track of problem add-ins in the registry via the Resiliency key. If Office thinks an add-in has an issue it will ask the user if they want to disable the add-in. If the user clicks Yes then it will be added to the DisabledItems key (see screenshot below).
Once an add-in has been placed in the DisabledItems key, Office will not let the add-in load by default. To get it to load, you must go to File > Options > Add-ins > Disabled Items > Go and then enable it there. Then close Outlook and continue with the registry settings below.
To solve this issue, ADD the addon (or whatever add-in you're using) must be added to the DoNotDisableAddinList with a DWORD value of 1 as shown in the screenshot.
The end path will vary slightly based on the version of Office and application so it is version 16 for our article below;
Below is an example of the location a Resiliency key for Word using Office 365.
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Word\Resiliency
Screenshot:
question: PowerPDF - Microsoft Word Add-ins not loading

answer:
The embedded object is bundled by COM, the Word program itself will remember the default program for opening embedded PDF document when user embed the PDF into word file. So, if the pdf file is embedded as Acrobat object, it cannot be opened with Kofax Power PDF and vice versa, the pdf file embedded as Kofax Power PDF object cannot be opened with Adobe Reader either.
Cause:
In this case, you insert the PDF document to Word when Kofax Power PDF is your default, so Word will remember to use only Kofax Power PDF to open the document. The recipient doesn't have Kofax Power PDF, so the error occurs.
SOLUTION:
If you insert the PDF into Word using Insert>Object >Package, it does not tag the PDF to force open into the embedding program.
In other words, it doesn't care if inserted while the PC had Acrobat or Kofax Power PDF and doesn't care if the reader trying to access it has Kofax Power PDF or Acrobat. It opens in any PDF program now.

Question:
What files are holding the settings for “Compatible with” option when changed to 1.4. The goal is to standardize all users settings from 1.5 to 1.4. Starting with a newly deployed Power PDF for the first time.
To address each PDF create template required read below.
Configuration Setup:
Configure the options you want for the PDF creation profiles within Power PDF, ensuring that everything aligns with your requirements.
After making these adjustments, be sure to close Power PDF or the PDF creation tool to save the settings.
Locating the Configuration File:
Navigate to the following directory to locate the file responsible for controlling these settings:
C:\Users\$account$\AppData\Roaming\Kofax\PDF\Profiles\ENU\Profile.ini
Within this file, look for the "Compatible" numbered value. If it's set to "1," this corresponds to PDF version 1.4 compatibility. Similarly, "0" denotes PDF version 1.3 compatibility. For instance, setting "Compatible=1" will ensure that the PDF creation profiles are configured to create PDFs with version 1.4 compatibility.
Deploying the Standard Configuration:
Once you've made the necessary changes to the Profile.ini file to achieve the desired PDF profile settings, you can now distribute this file to all users who need to adhere to the standard quality settings or any other specific configuration.
By following these steps, you can customize the PDF creation profiles and ensure that they meet the user's workflow needs. This approach helps maintain consistency across user and group policies.

To have Power PDF always set to printing on the long edge, it is not enough to set this in Power PDF print properties, it must be configured at the printer driver level to enable duplex with the flip on the long edge setting.
NOTE:
There is no Power PDF registry key or ini file setting to always enable long edge for duplex printing.
Power PDF pulls the setting this way to 'preset' the method of duplex printing as long as it is enabled at the device driver, however if the device driver does not have it enabled, when Power PDF enables it, the radio selection point is set to the short edge rather than the long edge by design.

Issue:
When setting the document profile default in Filesite (or Desksite) to override with using a specific author for new documents in imanage, Power PDF sets this incorrectly as the current operator is disregards this configuration.
Cause:
This setting is common when Assistants are working on behalf of an Attorney as the workflow requires; and default setting inside of powerPDF applies when no override exists to use the operator as the default author; which means we do not allow iManage to override this as it does for office applications and other PDF products out of the box.
Solution:
Create a setting override file which sets the DMS profile to allow iManage to override the default author; if required inside of PowerPDF too.
This is done using the following step; first create a file and save as PDFProDMS.ini, Include the following lines
[WorkSite]
IMPORT_PREFILL=0
Save this to C:\ProgramData\Kofax\PDFProDMS.ini
Once this is set; powerPDF pulls the author and metadata in use from the imanage default document profile as configured; and this setting can be pushed out to all users with the requirement to resolve the problem.

answer:
Here are the basic steps to password protect a PDF document in Nuance PowerPDF v1.1
Open the document in Nuance PowerPDF v1.1 and select the Security tab from the menus.
Select Security on the right to open the Security panel.
Double Click on "Interactive" to assign security to this document.
You will see a message asking "are you sure you want to change the security settings of this document?" - click OK to continue.
The Password Security Options window will open. Check the box by "Allow document opening only by password", Set a password in the Document Open Password window.
A window will open up and require you to verify the password - enter the same password again.
A message will come up verifying security has been set on this document.
Once the document is saved, you will have to enter the password to open the document.
There are additional options available for passwords.
Power PDF allows you to place password protection on the following items:
PDF documents
Envelopes containing a set of PDF files and optionally other types of file
Packages prepared by PDF Create containing a set of PDF files
Portfolios prepared by Power PDF containing PDF files and optionally other types of file.
PDF files in envelopes, packages and portfolios may have their own passwords.
The advantage of placing a password on an envelope or a portfolio is that it offers protection to non-PDF files that themselves cannot be protected by passwords.
Note: Passwords cannot be applied to documents containing a digital signature.
There are two types of password:
Open Password
People with this password can open the document. If the document has no permissions password, the PDF, once opened can be used freely. If it has a permissions password set, a person with only the Open password can view the set of permitted and prohibited actions but cannot modify them.
Permissions Password
People with this password can open the document, view its permitted and prohibited actions and modify or clear them, also change passwords or remove one or both of the passwords.
Power PDF has no particular requirements for passwords; a good password contains at least six characters, is not easily guessed and has a mix of letters, numbers and symbols. Password use demands careful management, without a valid password the document can no longer be opened.
For more information, please consult the help menus within the PowerPDF program.
A Word document with screenshots of the settings is attached that can be distributed to clients.
Attachments:
How to password protect a PDF in Nuance PowerPDF v1.1.docx (158 KB)

question: Power PDF integration toolbar not showing up in Internet Explorer 11

answer:
This can happen when the original is a very rare type of non-standard formatted document. This issue is because this file contains fonts with "non-standard encoding". A program without OCR cannot recognize the correct character codes, which is why converting the document to an Editable document shows strange characters. Adobe Acrobat has the same issues with this document.
Here is a workaround to enable you to edit documents like this:
From the HOME tab, click on "Make PDF Searchable" and select "Make PDF Searchable".
In the Convert Pages Window, click on Settings.
Uncheck Keep original images if it is checked.
Under Process Pages, uncheck "Process documents using OCR" and check "Recognize non-standard encoded pages". It should look like the attached screenshot, "Searchable PDF Conversion Settings.png"
Click "OK" to save the settings and then click "OK" again to process the document.
Now you should be able to select the "Edit" tab, "Convert to Editable", "Document" and "Convert" and it will create a legible editable document.
Attachments:
Searchable PDF Conversion Settings.png (61 KB)

This executable is part of the "Watched Folders and Scan Inboxes" feature in PowerPDF. A Scan Inbox is a specific type of watched folder: to browse to the scan target folder of your scanning device and identify it as your scan inbox to receive notifications on incoming files.
You set the scan inbox folder at File --> Options --> Automation --> Watched Folder. Screenshot is attached to this article.
There is no automatic file conversion involved in this functionality. When you access your incoming scans you open them from this dedicated folder or browse this folder from inside Power PDF.
When this functionality is enabled in Power PDF, the inboxmonitor.exe is the program that runs in the taskbar to monitor the defined inbox and provide the notification when a new file arrives. It is located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Nuance\Power PDF.
If you do not want the inboxmonitor.exe running, you can disable the scanbox notification by simply unchecking "Enable Scan Inbox Notification" in Options - Automation. Or, it is controlled by this registry key:
Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00
[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Nuance\PDF\V1\Inboxes\ScanToDesktop]
"Notify"=dword:00000000
0 = disable / 1 = enable

question: ERROR "Registrycontroller.exe has stopped working" when starting up Power PDF 2.0

answer:
This is a known issue with OLE embedded objects with MS Office. When you embed an object into an Office Document, Microsoft embeds the program information for the source program. So, if you embed a file from Power PDF, you must have Power PDF to open the file. You will have the same issue with any PDF program that is not Adobe if the recipient only has Adobe Reader. You would see the same error message if you double-click a bitmap image object in a Word document on a workstation that does not have MS Paint.
You can google that error message and see more information on this from Microsoft. Unfortunately, there is nothing we can do about that on our end, other than recommend avoiding embedded objects. The client should consider using a PDF portfolio that contains the Word document and the PDF.

Question:
How to Manually add Nuance PDF printer back if accidently deleted from Windows Devices and Printers screen
Workflow to reproduce:
The User went into windows control panel, and then into "Devices and Printers" view and displayed their currently installed printers and right clicked on "Nuance PDF' and deleted the PDF Printer from their system. They but did so accidently, and now would like to PDF print functionality back inside of their Power PDF software and is unable to fix this manually.
Solution:
Manual reinstallation of the Nuance PDF printer is not possible inside of the windows "add printer" wizard. Our software must be reinstallated or repaired.
Option 1:
The user will have to go into the "Program and features" control panel MMC snap in and then click to uninstall Power PDF and follow through the prompts to choose to "modify/Repair the power PDF installation and click next, and this step will recopy and resolve this issue with the missing and corrupt files as well as shortcuts and to re add the Nuance PDF printer.
Option 2:
The user will have to go into root of the local c: drive when the Power PDF where the installation origanally was extracted to and launch the setup application to start the process to "modify/Repair" the power PDF installation and click next, and this step will re-copy and resolve this issue with the missing and corrupt files as well as shortcuts and to re add the Nuance PDF printer.
This path is located for example:
A) Power PDF 1.2 Advanced volume will be this path : c:\ppdf-12-av-efgdiswabtmjkpryznch-15356.100\setup.exe

question: Nuance toolbars missing in IE 11

answer:
The embeded object is bundled by COM, the Word program itself will remember the default program for opening embeded PDF document when user embed the PDF into word file. So if the pdf file is embeded as Acrobat object, it can not be opened with Nuance and vice versa, the pdf file embeded as Nuance object can not be opened with Adobe Reader either.
In this case, you insert the PDF document to Word when Nuance is your default, so Word will remembers to use only Nuance to open the document. The recipient doesn't have Nuance, so the error occurs.
If you insert the PDF into Word using Insert>Object >Package, it does not tag the PDF to force open into the embedding program. In other words, it doesn't care if inserted while the PC had Acrobat or Nuance, and doesn't care if the reader trying to access it has Nuance or Acrobat. It opens in any PDF program.

question: PDF Redaction removes highlighting if any of the highlighting is in the redacted area

answer:
The embeded object is bundled by COM, the Excel program itself will remember the default program for opening embeded PDF document when user embeds the PDF into Excel file. So if the PDF file is embeded as an Acrobat object, it can not be opened with Power PDF and vice versa, the PDF file embedded as Power PDF object can not be opened with Adobe Reader either.
In this case, you insert the PDF document to Excel when Power PDF is your default, so Excel will remembers to use only Power PDF to open the document. The recipient doesn't have Power PDF, so the error occurs.
If you insert the PDF into Excel using Insert > Object >Package, it does not tag the PDF to force open into the embedding program. In other words, it doesn't care if inserted while the PC had Acrobat or Power PDF, and doesn't care if the reader trying to access it has Power PDF or Acrobat. It opens in any PDF program.

answer:
Problem Statement:
Helvetica font changes to Arial when the document is printed to a PDF
Cause:
There are five (5) standard fonts in PDF that are not embedded by PDF applications by default. The assumption was that these are standard fonts and everyone already has them on their computer.
These are the fonts that are not embedded by default:
Times
(regular, italic, bold, and bold italic)
Courier
(regular, oblique, bold and bold oblique)
Helvetica
(regular, oblique, bold and bold oblique)
Symbol
Zapf Dingbats
This is a MAC vs Windows difference. Helvetica is a standard font on the Mac, equivalent of Arial. Helvetica does not exist on Windows by default, so when a Mac generated PDF is opened on a Windows machine and conversion is done, it will replace Helvetica with something else. The closest font to that on a Windows system will be Arial so that is what will be used.
Most PDF applications have an override like Power PDF on Windows to allow a create of a PDF to override the default Embed Non-Standard Fonts and Embed all Fonts when a PDF is saved. This setting is called Font Embed in the Power PDF Create Assistant settings under profiles.

answer:
By default, Power PDF uses your login name as the Author in comments you add to a PDF, You can change the Author of the comments by performing the following steps:
Open a PDF document in Power PDF.
Go to Option -> Comment -> Commenting
Disable "Always use Log-in Name for Author name"
Add a comment, or if there is an existing comment, right-click on the comment and select Properties.
Go to the Display Tab and add the Authors name you want to appear on the comments.
Close the dialog box.
Right Click on the comment again and select "Set Current Properties as Default".
From this point on, Power PDF will use the Author name you added in all new comments.

answer:
This function was added in Power PDF hotfix after version 3.0 and can be used with kofax Power PDF 4.0, 4.1 and later releases.
This hotfix implemented the ability to force the "iManage - Use Document Profile" option from an ini-file, separately for Open and Save dialogs.
This is defined in the file "PDFProDMS.ini" to be created in "C:\ProgramData\Nuance\", and the content should be as following:
[WorkSite]
FORCE_USEPROFILE_SAVE=1
FORCE_USEPROFILE_OPEN=0
The choices are:
0 - do not force this option; 1 - force it

How to change the default filter conditions when opening a document from iManage WorkSite / DeskSite. When you select Open from DMS, is there a way to default the highlighted area to all file types instead of just PDF?
Use the following steps.
Create the following file: %ALLUSERSPROFILE%\Nuance\PDFProDMS.ini (usually C:\ProgramData\Nuance\PDFProDMS.ini)
2. Add the following to the PDFProDMS.ini file to display all files.
[WorkSite]
PDFOPENFILTERAPPID=""
If you just want to see PDF and WORD files, use this:
[WorkSite]
PDFOPENFILTERAPPID="ACROBAT, WORD, WORDX"
If you want to include other specific file types, you just have to include the Alias as shown in the filter dropdown in the iManage interface such as EXCEL for spreadsheets.

This is by design - the only way to preserve the annotations is by printing the Word document to a PDF with the markups visible in Word.
In order for annotations to be preserved and viewable after a document has been converted from MS Word format to PDF, the following must be performed:
In the MS Word document, make sure the annotations are visible - if they are not, On the Review tab, enable Show Markup
Click File > Print
From the Printer drop-down box, select ScanSoft PDF Create! or Gaaiho PDF or Nuance PDF.
Click Print
In the Save As window, designate a location to which the file will be saved and enter a name for the file
Click Save and the resulting PDF will include the annotations.

Problem:
Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge are opening PDF files in the browser despite Power PDF being set as the default.
Solution:
By design Chromium based browsers will always open PDF files natively regardless of the OS settings. You can now disable this behavior in the web browsers.
Please note: The settings below are for third party products not manufactured by Kofax, please verify all steps with the manufacture before making changes.
Chrome Settings Automatically Opening PDFs in Power PDF
Ensure that Power PDF is installed on the computer and set as the default application for PDFs.
In Chrome, go to the 
Menu
 icon, then choose 
Settings
.
In the 
Privacy and Security
section, select 
Site Settings
.
Select 
Additional content settings
.
Scroll down and select 
PDF documents
.
Switch the 
Download PDF files instead of automatically opening them in Chrome
 to 
On
.
PDF files will download in Chrome just like any other file. You can select the downloaded file at the bottom of the window, then choose 
Always open with system viewer
, then the file will open in the default PDF viewer.
Edge Settings Automatically Opening PDFs in Power PDF
Ensure that Power PDF is installed on the computer and set as the default application for PDFs.
In Edge, go to the 
Menu
 icon, then choose 
Settings
.
In the Cookies and Site Permissions

section, select 
PDF Documents
.
Select 
Additional content settings
.
Switch the 
Always download PDF files
to 
On
.
PDF files will download in Chrome just like any other file. You can select the downloaded file at the bottom of the window, then choose 
Always open with system viewer
, then the file will open in the default PDF viewer.
Depending on your Windows setup you may be prompted to open in the system viewer on your first PDF file.

answer:
Problem:
When clicking on File/Share/E-mail, Outlook opens instead of Thunderbird, even when Mozilla Thunderbird is set to the default e-mail client, and wants to send PDFs by mail via Thunderbird.
Solution:
Power PDF is looking for the following registry hive HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\OUTLOOK.exe.
So, as long as this registry entry is on the system, Power PDF will try to launch Outlook, regardless of what email client is installed and/or set as the default mail application in Windows.
This seems to be hard coded into Power PDF, and cannot be changed. However, I have found 2 workarounds other than uninstalling Outlook that have worked on a test system.
Option 1:
Uninstall Outlook.
Option 2:
The following will prevent Outlook from being used.
Rename C:\Program Files\Microsoft Office\root\Office16\OUTLOOK.EXE to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Office\root\Office16\OUTLOOK.EXE.old
Option 3:
The following will allow Outlook to be used, but will allow Power PDF to use the default email client.
Rename the following registry hive from:
HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\OUTLOOK.exe
to
HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\App Paths\XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX_OUTLOOK.EXE

answer:
When logging is needed for Power PDF 5.0, log files for both x32 and x64 bit processing should be collected.
32-Bit:
To enable x32 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
then rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the installation folder which is typically "C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 50".
In some cases, renaming this file will require admin rights, but this depends on the customers environment
64-Bit:
To enable x64 bit logging,
first remove/rename RECDIAG_LOG.ini which is 1kb in size
rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' to 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file found in the x64 folder within the installation folder which is typically " C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\Power PDF 50\x64".
Once the 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' file is in place in both locations, create a folder named 'RECDIAG' in the C:\TEMP folder. If this folder is not manually created before testing, the logs will not be created (C:\temp\recdiag).
Power PDF should start writing logs to C:\temp\recdiag as soon as the software is launched.
When testing is complete, be sure to rename the file 'RECDIAG_LOG.ini' back to 'RECDIAG_LOG.in_' so Power PDF does not continue logging.
Level of Complexity
Moderate

answer:
Problem:
If there is a smart card from the European Patent Office in use to insert signatures in PDF documents, those signed documents are no longer accepted by the Federal Patent Office for document submissions because of a recently backend change by the Federal Patent Office.
Solution:
To make the signed documents conform with the backend change of the Federal Patent Office, the default signing format needs to be changed to CAdES-Equivalent.
Signature options are stored in "%appdata%\Kofax\PDF\PowerPDF\Preferences\Security.ini" file.
Configure Power PDF security setting the way above, then save this and replace the "Security.ini" in new/other installations.

Power PDF stores any of the users signatures that are handwritten in one folder within a PDF file, and this can be copied from one workstation/profile as they migrate systems.
